issue.body:
Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open the Notes tab.
2. Switch to another tab.
3. Observe the label of the previously opened tab.

Expected Behavior:
The previous tab should keep its original label.

Actual Behavior:
The label of the previous tab changes to match the currently selected tab.
issue.title: [Bug] Tab label changes incorrectly when switching tabs

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
- Move pnpm/action-setup before setup-node so node cache can detect pnpm
- Replace fragile sleep 10 with proper Docker health checks for MongoDB and Redis
- Add --forceExit to backend tests to prevent hanging open connections
- Add --passWithNoTests to frontend tests to avoid failure with no test files
- Add shamefully-hoist=true and strict-peer-dependencies=false to .npmrc for NestJS monorepo compatibility
- Remove ui:tui from turbo.json (crashes in non-interactive CI terminals)
- Add test task to turbo.json with cache disabled (tests use live services)
pull_requests_reviewed.title: fix(ci): fix pnpm setup order, service health checks, and turbo config
[testing] make in very module folder called test contiune unit , 2e2 …
refactor(menu): separate menu feature into its own module
feat(workspace) : add state into the context of workspace ^GctiveNoteI…

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
## Description
Completed the missing CRUD operations, soft delete, and favorite toggle functionalities inside the `Nodes` module as requested. 

### Changes Made:
- **Controller Fix:** Resolved a recursive stack overflow bug in the `@Delete(':id')` endpoint by correctly routing it to call the service's `sortDelete` method instead of self-invoking.
- **Toggle Favorite Functionality:** Implemented a new `@Patch(':id/favorite')` endpoint in the controller and added corresponding logic in `NodesService.handlefavorite()` to dynamically toggle the favorite status of a node document in Mongoose.

## Verification & Testing
- Verified compilation and server start via `npm run start:dev`.
- Created and tested a complete Postman collection verifying that all updated endpoints work correctly with JWT authentication middleware.

Closes #11
pull_requests_reviewed.title: Feature/nodes crud endpoints

pull_requests_reviewed.body:
- Add custom Modal component for UI consistency
- Prompt user for node title upon creation (note/folder)
- Enable renaming nodes directly from sidebar and main view context menus
- Implement PUT /nodes/:id endpoint in backend for title updates
Closes #4
pull_requests_reviewed.title: feat: implement node naming and renaming via custom modal (#4)
